mnemonics: DAD: Dynamic Action Depends on the actual object's class, not the reference type.
keyPoints: Dynamic Method Dispatch is the mechanism by which a call to an overridden method is resolved at runtime, based on the type of the object being referred to.
It is the core principle behind Java's runtime polymorphism.
It only works with inherited methods that are overridden, and it requires using a superclass reference variable.
commonMistakes: Trying to use dispatch with static or final methods, which cannot be overridden.
Attempting to call a subclass-specific method using a superclass reference without casting, leading to compile-time errors.
Confusing compile-time (method overloading) and runtime (method overriding) polymorphism.
